channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Las Vegas man found guilty of murder, abandoning victim's body in barrel
item.description: A Las Vegas jury found a man guilty Thursday of killing a man and disposing of his body in a barrel, a court spokesperson said.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, NewsNation

item.title: Gene Hackman and his wife tested negative for carbon monoxide, sheriff says
item.description: SANTA FE, N.M. (AP) — Preliminary autopsy results didn’t determine how Oscar-winner Gene Hackman and his wife died at their home in Santa Fe, New Mexico, but did rule out that they were killed by carbon monoxide poisoning, the sheriff leading the investigation said Friday.
item.dc:creator: SUSAN MONTOYA BRYAN, JACQUES BILLEAUD and JOHN SEEWER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Utah senators react to contentious Oval Office meeting between Trump and Zelenskyy
item.description: Utah's senators are responding to a contentious interaction between Trump and Vance and Zelenskyy Friday with contrasting public statements.
item.dc:creator: Aubree B. Jennings, ABC 4

item.title: Zelensky won’t apologize for Oval Office spat, says it was bad for both sides
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky wouldn’t apologize for the meeting with President Trump, which erupted into a remarkable and tense spat, arguing that the situation was bad for both countries.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Actor Gene Hackman's death not from carbon monoxide, authorities say
item.description: Authorities investigating the deaths of legendary actor Gene Hackman and his wife Betsy Arakawa say they have ruled out carbon monoxide poisoning as the cause.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, Fox 5

item.title: Texas GOP leaders defend appointee after tense exchange over diversity hiring
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — An appointee of Texas Gov. Greg Abbott wiped away tears and gave an emotional defense of her credentials after a tense exchange over diversity hiring policies, which was later followed by the state's top Republicans rushing to support her publicly.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Two NYC window washers go for wild ride 78 stories up before being rescued by FDNY
item.description: Two window washers in New York City were pulled to safety 78 floors up after their scaffolding broke, sending them on a wild ride that blew them around, smashing into the building.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Texas measles cases rise to 146 in an outbreak that led to a child's death
item.description: DALLAS (AP) — The number of people with measles in Texas increased to 146 in an outbreak that led this week to the death of an unvaccinated school-aged child, health officials said Friday. The number of cases — Texas’ largest in nearly 30 years — increased by 22 since Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: JAMIE STENGLE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Iowa's governor signs a bill removing gender identity protections from the state’s civil rights code
item.description: DES MOINES, Iowa (AP) — Iowa became the first U.S. state to remove gender identity protections from its civil rights code on Friday when Gov. Kim Reynolds signed into law a bill that opponents say will expose transgender people and other Iowans to discrimination in all aspects of daily life.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH FINGERHUT, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump-Zelensky clash highlights Congress at a crossroads over foreign policy
item.description: The explosive clash between President Trump and Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky in the White House on Friday has sparked a firestorm on Capitol Hill, pitting “America First” Republicans against Ukraine backers from both parties in the ferocious battle over the future of U.S. foreign policy.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, WGN 9

item.title: Alabama governor commutes death row inmate Rocky Myers' sentence to life in prison
item.description: Alabama Gov. Kay Ivey has commuted the death sentence of Robin “Rocky” Myers to life in prison after noting questions about his case.
item.dc:creator: Kim Chandler, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Peak bloom for the iconic cherry blossom trees of Washington, DC, is predicted for late March
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — For decades, the thousands of blooming cherry blossom trees in the nation’s capital have served as both a magnet to visitors and the unofficial start of the tourist season for Washington, D.C.
item.dc:creator: ASHRAF KHALIL, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Firings at US weather and oceans agency risk lives and economy, former agency heads warn
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The federal weather and oceans agency touches people's daily lives in unnoticed ways, so massive firings there will likely cause needless deaths and a big hit to America's economy, according to the people who ran it.
item.dc:creator: SETH BORENSTEIN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Pennsylvania officer's last act after he was shot was to rescue a colleague, police chief says
item.description: A police officer who was shot to death while responding to an armed man with hostages inside a Pennsylvania hospital intensive care unit was remembered Friday as a dedicated public servant who did not hesitate to run toward danger and died a hero.
item.dc:creator: MARK SCOLFORO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Missouri House reviews bill to ban NDAs in child abuse cases
item.description: The bill being considered is aiming to provide a "thread of hope" for survivors and prevent further silence and trauma.
item.dc:creator: Parker Padgett, NewsNation

item.title: Wyoming's only full-service clinic stops providing abortions after new regulations signed
item.description: CHEYENNE, Wyol (AP) — Wyoming's only full-service abortion clinic stopped providing abortions Friday, but it remained open after Gov. Mark Gordon approved a law requiring that such facilities be licensed as surgical centers.
item.dc:creator: MEAD GRUVER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Microsoft shutting down Skype in May
item.description: Microsoft is closing down Skype, the video-calling service it bought for $8.5 billion in 2011, which had helped spark a transformation in how people communicate online.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Gene Hackman, wife tested negative for carbon monoxide, sheriff says
item.description: Actor Gene Hackman, his wife Betsy Arakawa, and their dog were found dead in their Santa Fe home, in a scene that one detective had deemed "suspicious enough in nature to require a thorough search and investigation.
item.dc:creator: Fallon Fischer, Fox 5

item.title: Leonard Peltier remains defiant in AP interview, maintaining innocence and vowing continued activism
item.description: BELCOURT, N.D. (AP) — More than 50 years after a shootout on the Pine Ridge Indian Reservation landed him in federal prison, Leonard Peltier remains defiant.
item.dc:creator: GRAHAM LEE BREWER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jury finds Illinois landlord guilty of murder, hate crime in 2023 attack on Palestinian American boy
item.description: A jury has found a 73-year-old Illinois landlord guilty in a hate crime attack that left a Palestinian American boy dead and injured his mother.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Tate brothers, who face human trafficking charges in Romania, are back in the US: What to know
item.description: Influencer brothers Andrew and Tristan Tate, who are charged with human trafficking in Romania, arrived Thursday in the U.S. after authorities lifted travel restrictions on the siblings, who have millions of online followers.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump says he plans to add White House ballroom, pave part of the Rose Garden
item.description: President Trump in a new interview said he is planning to add a ballroom to the White House and to pave part the Rose Garden, similar to a patio at his Mar-a-Lago property in Florida.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Cardinal McElroy calls for compassion in immigration policy as he prepares to lead in Washington
item.description: Cardinal Robert McElroy, who is getting ready to take over as archbishop of Washington, D.C. in March, called for compassion and dignity for migrants Thursday.
item.dc:creator: DEEPA BHARATH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: EPA backtracks on Trump comment about cutting staff by 65% but says major spending cuts are coming
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Despite a comment by President Donald Trump, the Environmental Protection Agency does not plan to reduce its staff by 65%, the White House and the agency said Thursday, though major budget cuts are likely.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW DALY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Man on 'Most Wanted' list for over 4 decades caught in New Mexico
item.description: After more than four decades, Stephen Craig Campbell, one of the United States' most wanted men, is now behind bars.
item.dc:creator: Bela Olague, KRON4

item.title: As Trump's deadline to eliminate DEI nears, few schools openly rush to make changes
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Schools and colleges across the U.S. face a Friday deadline to end diversity programs or risk having their federal money pulled by the Trump administration, yet few are openly rushing to make changes.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, JOCELYN GECKER and CHEYANNE MUMPHREY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Gene Hackman was more than an everyman: An Appreciation
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — One of the greatest American actors of the 20th century was voted “least likely to succeed” by his first theater school, wasn’t a star until he was 40 and possessed a face he once described as “your everyday mineworker.
item.dc:creator: JAKE COYLE, Associated Press, ABC 4
